summary: This is a short status update post from Joel Spolsky covering several operational issues at Fog Creek Software. The server keeps crashing due to overheating, prompting an upgrade to Dell rack servers. A mailing list bug caused duplicate emails during the crashes. The CityDesk beta has launched and is already surfacing bugs that months of internal testing missed. The discussion forums have a clever trick for tracking unread topics without server-side state.
key_points: Fog Creek's home-built server kept crashing from overheating, highlighting the limits of DIY infrastructure for a growing company
The CityDesk beta immediately surfaced bugs that months of internal testing and 329 bug fixes had missed, validating the importance of external beta testing
A stateless UI trick for tracking read/unread forum topics demonstrates clever engineering within constraints
money_quotes: I guess it's time to replace it with a real industrial strength server…
We've been banging on this dang thing for months. We fixed 329 bugs. Then Brad W. downloads it, plays for ten seconds, and finds another.
key_insight: No matter how thoroughly you test internally, real users will find bugs within seconds of touching your software, which is exactly why you do a beta.
